title: I built a complete Hotel & Restaurant POS system from scratch — here's the story.
content:
I built a complete Hotel & Restaurant POS system from scratch — here's the story.

6 months ago, I walked into a restaurant and watched the owner juggle 4 different apps just to take an order, track inventory, and generate an invoice.

A notepad for orders. WhatsApp for the kitchen. An Excel sheet for stock. A calculator for bills.

I thought — what if one system could replace all of that?

That question turned into RestoCaptain — a full-stack, production-ready Point of Sale ecosystem designed specifically for hotels and restaurants.

Not a weekend project. Not a tutorial clone. A real system that's deployed, tested, and live today.

Here's the story of how I built it. 👇

🧩 The Problem I Set Out to Solve

The Indian hospitality industry is massive — over 7.5 million restaurants and growing. Yet most small-to-mid-sized hotels still rely on fragmented tools:

  • Paper-based order taking that leads to errors

  • No real-time visibility into stock levels

  • Manual billing with no invoice history

  • Zero analytics to understand what's actually selling

I didn't want to build another "food ordering app." I wanted to build the operating system for a restaurant.

🏗️ The Architecture — Three Apps, One Ecosystem

RestoCaptain isn't a single application. It's a three-app ecosystem where each piece serves a distinct user and purpose:

1. 🎛️ Admin Dashboard — The command center. This is where the restaurant owner lives. Managing menus with drag-and-drop reordering, processing real-time orders from multiple tables, generating QR codes for each table, handling invoices with PDF export, managing table and room bookings with a visual calendar, tracking revenue through interactive charts, staff management with role-based invitations, and a social media integration panel — all in one place.

🔗 Live: https://hotel-pos-amdin-v2.vercel.app/

2. 📱 Customer App — The diner's experience. Customers scan a QR code on their table and instantly see the restaurant's digital menu. No app download needed. They can browse categories, add items to cart, customize orders, place them directly to the kitchen, and even book tables or rooms — all from their phone's browser.

🔗 Live: https://hotel-pos-customer-v2.vercel.app/e2c1bfed-17e3-474e-a53d-3fe137a24b24?table=1

3. 📦 Stock Management System — The back-office brain. This is where waste gets tracked, purchases get recorded, suppliers get managed, recipes get costed, and stock counts get reconciled. It has 110+ features including daily usage tracking, waste analysis, purchase order workflows, reorder suggestions, and multi-location support.

🔗 Live: https://hotel-pos-stock-management-v2.vercel.app/dashboard

All three apps share a single Supabase backend with real-time subscriptions — so when a customer places an order, the admin dashboard lights up instantly. When stock is consumed, the inventory system reflects it in real time.

🛠️ The Tech Stack — Modern, Scalable, Production-Ready

I chose every tool in this stack deliberately:

  • Next.js 15 (App Router) — Server components, server actions, streaming SSR

  • React 19 — Latest concurrent features

  • TypeScript — End-to-end type safety across all three apps

  • Supabase — Auth, PostgreSQL database, Row-Level Security, real-time subscriptions, storage

  • MUI (Material UI) v7 — Enterprise-grade component library

  • TailwindCSS v4 — Utility-first styling with a custom design system

  • Recharts — Interactive data visualizations and analytics

  • Framer Motion — Smooth, polished micro-animations

  • Dexie.js (IndexedDB) — Offline-first data caching

  • jsPDF — Client-side PDF invoice generation

  • PWA Support — Installable on any device

  • Vitest + Playwright — Unit tests and end-to-end testing

  • Deployed on Vercel — CI/CD with automatic preview deployments

✨ Features I'm Most Proud Of

Real-time Order Flow Customer scans QR → browses menu → places order → Admin sees it live → Kitchen processes it → Customer tracks status. All in real-time. No page refresh needed.

QR Code Table System Each table gets a unique QR code generated from the admin panel. Scanning it pre-fills the table number and links the order to the right context. Simple, but it eliminates a whole class of errors.

Drag-and-Drop Menu Management Owners can reorder menu categories and items with drag-and-drop. It sounds simple, but getting DnD to work with server state, optimistic updates, and mobile touch events was one of the hardest problems I solved.

Comprehensive Stock Management 110+ features including: items CRUD with CSV/Excel import-export, purchase recording with history, stock level monitoring with low-stock alerts, daily usage and waste tracking, supplier management, recipe management with auto-cost calculation, purchase order workflows, physical stock count with variance reports, reorder suggestions based on PAR levels, and multi-location inventory tracking.

Invoice System with PDF Export Generate professional invoices with tax breakdowns, export to PDF, and maintain a searchable invoice history.

Room & Table Booking System Hotels can manage both room bookings and table reservations through a unified calendar interface, complete with status tracking and customer notifications.

Modern Enterprise Design System A custom "Slate & Indigo" design system with Inter typography, carefully curated color palettes, glassmorphism effects, and consistent micro-animations. Every screen looks like it belongs to a premium SaaS product.

📖 Lessons Learned

1. Real-time is harder than it looks. Supabase real-time subscriptions are powerful, but handling race conditions, optimistic updates, and reconnection logic took more effort than building the features themselves.

2. Offline-first isn't optional for restaurants. Internet goes down in a busy kitchen. IndexedDB caching with Dexie.js ensures the system keeps working even when the connection drops.

3. Multi-tenancy changes everything. Supporting multiple organizations with Row-Level Security in PostgreSQL means every query, every policy, every migration has to account for data isolation. It's not something you bolt on later.

4. Design is product. I invested heavily in the UI/UX — not because it's fun (it is), but because restaurant staff have zero patience for clunky interfaces during a dinner rush. The UI has to be instant and intuitive.

5. Testing gives you courage. With Vitest for unit tests and Playwright for end-to-end flows, I can refactor aggressively without fear. The test suite catches regressions before they reach production.

🚀 What's Next

The roadmap includes:

  • AI-powered demand forecasting using historical usage data

  • Accounting integration with Tally and Zoho Books

  • Barcode/QR scanning for faster stock counting

  • Native mobile app with offline stock counting

  • POS integration for auto-deducting ingredients on sale

💡 Why I'm Sharing This

I built RestoCaptain not just as a project, but as proof that a single developer, armed with modern tools and a clear vision, can build production-grade software that solves real problems.

If you're a developer — I hope this inspires you to build something that matters. If you're in hospitality — I'd love to hear what pain points you face daily. If you're hiring — this is how I think, design, and ship.

Try it live:

🎛️ Admin: https://hotel-pos-amdin-v2.vercel.app/

📱 Customer: https://hotel-pos-customer-v2.vercel.app/e2c1bfed-17e3-474e-a53d-3fe137a24b24?table=1

📦 Stock: https://hotel-pos-stock-management-v2.vercel.app/dashboard

📧 Login: sportsrizzz@gmail.com
